---
title: My dynamic report
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE, fig.width = 6, fig.height = 4)

```

```{css}
body {
  text-align: center;
  background-color: #ffe6f3;
  font-family: Candara
}

h2 {
  color: #ffe6f3;
  margin-top: 40px;
  background-color: #ff66b3;
}

```

## TikTok Ad Engagement Analysis

I chose to analyse what was advertised on TikTok.

Over the course of my logging period I tracked the topic of the ad, the amount of time spent watching the ad and if the advertisers TikTok account was accidentally opened.

Below I have graphs showing the number of ads encountered per topic (Fig.1), and the mean watch time per ad topic (Fig.2).

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
logged_data <- read_csv("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vQfV2kGJsFLMRpegD8AiXaSp60rygqs1_tbH2X1HfZhR9aRYcvQQw4ZWUjN3Uu_ErM1H1KWIG_1mqxw/pub?gid=2136539218&single=true&output=csv")

latest_data <- logged_data %>%
  rename(
    topic = "What was the main topic of the ad?",
    watch_time = "How long did you watch the ad in seconds?",
    accidental_swipe = "Did you accidentally open the advertisers account?"
  ) %>%
  select(topic, watch_time, accidental_swipe) %>%
  mutate(
    topic = factor(topic, levels = c(
      "Beauty", "Clothing", "Food", "Technology", "Other"
    ))
  )

mean_time <- latest_data$watch_time %>%
  mean() %>%
  round(1)

total_watch_time <- latest_data$watch_time %>%
  sum()

total_responses <- latest_data %>%
  nrow()

ggplot(data = latest_data) +
  geom_bar(aes(topic, fill = topic)) +
  labs(title = "Number of advertisements per topic",
       x = "Ad topic",
       y = "Number of ads",
       caption = "Fig 1: Total ads observed per topic") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Beauty" = "#ffcce6",
    "Clothing" = "#ff80c1",
    "Food" = "#ff339c",
    "Technology" = "#e60077",
    "Other" = "#99004f"
  ))

latest_data %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_bar(aes(x = topic, y = watch_time, fill = topic), stat = "summary", fun = "mean") +
  labs(title = "Mean watch time per ad topic",
       x = "Ad topic",
       y = "Mean watch time (seconds)",
       caption = "Fig 2: Mean watch time of ads per topic") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Beauty" = "#ffcce6",
    "Clothing" = "#ff80c1",
    "Food" = "#ff339c",
    "Technology" = "#e60077",
    "Other" = "#99004f"
  ))

```


On average, a TikTok ad is watched for **`r mean_time` seconds**.

In total, the time spent watching TikTok ads is **`r total_watch_time` seconds**.

The total number of responses is **`r total_responses`**.

![This is how I felt gathering data](https://c.tenor.com/Xm1L7-otNRwAAAAC/tenor.gif)
